title: Today's AI Brief: We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including an interview with the author of 'The Future of Truth' and the return of the AI Hype Index.
imageAlt: Image from source article for We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well
imageCaption: We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well
imageSourceTitle: We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've been digging into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence, and I'm excited to share them with you. It's been a busy week, and I've got a few stories that caught my eye.

First up, WIRED published an excerpt from Steve Rosenbaum's new book, The Future of Truth, which explores how AI is warping people's sense of reality. When they followed up with an interview to discuss how he used AI in his research, things didn't quite go as planned. It seems the author struggled to explain his process, which is, well, a bit ironic given the topic of his book. I mean, if you're writing about the impact of AI on truth, you'd think you'd have a solid grasp on how it works, right?

Next, climate tech companies are going public, and the AI Hype Index is back. My read is that this index is a fun way to track the ups and downs of AI enthusiasm, but it's also a reminder that the field is moving fast and can be unpredictable. According to MIT Technology Review, solar and battery companies are leading the charge in climate tech IPOs. It's interesting to see how these companies are using AI to drive innovation and efficiency in their operations.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
